commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=1cd82a718b6e748407ca6f66fbac2c667ecdd715)
commands.review_summary:
The branch imports `readProviderJsonResponse` into the shared OpenAI-compatible image provider, uses it before parsing successful image JSON, and updates the test mock.

PR surface: Source +5, Tests +5. Total +10 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main calls `response.json()` on a provider-controlled OpenAI-compatible image success body. I did not run a provider-path repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainers need to settle the image JSON cap policy or ask for a cap-sized revision before this PR can land.

target.title: fix(image-generation): replace unbounded response.json() with readProviderJsonResponse
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`src/image-generation/openai-compatible-image-provider.ts:272` calls `await response.json()` with no byte-level cap. A hostile or malfunctioning image generation endpoint (or an intermediary) can return an oversized JSON body that exhausts process memory during JSON parsing.

This is the same bounded-read pattern Alix-007 applied to 15+ other providers (`readProviderJsonResponse`, 16 MiB cap). Image generation was one of the remaining unbounded `.json()` call sites.

## Changes

- `src/image-generation/openai-compatible-image-provider.ts:272` — replace `parseOpenAiCompatibleImageResponse(await response.json(), ...)` with `readProviderJsonResponse` + `parseOpenAiCompatibleImageResponse(parsedBody, ...)`. Adds `readProviderJsonResponse` to the existing SDK import.
- `src/image-generation/openai-compatible-image-provider.test.ts` — add `readProviderJsonResponse` to the `openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-http` mock definition so the new import resolves in tests.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: unbounded `response.json()` on image generation HTTP responses; after the fix reads are capped at 16 MiB.
- **Real environment tested**: a real `node:http` server bound to `127.0.0.1` returning a JSON body exceeding 16 MiB (20 MiB), plus a 1 MiB negative-control body, plus a valid OpenAI-compatible image response. Drives the production `readProviderJsonResponse` and `parseOpenAiCompatibleImageResponse` over a real TCP socket. Node v22.22.0.
- **Exact steps**: `node --import tsx _proof_image_gen_json.mts` (proof script kept local-only, not committed).
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```
  === image-generation JSON response bounded read (cap=16777216) ===

  PASS  Image generation JSON bounded: overflow; bytesSent=20971709; server.aborted=true
  PASS  Image generation JSON negative control: small body parsed; cap did not trigger
  PASS  Image generation JSON happy path: OpenAI-compatible response parsed (images created)

  === All image-generation JSON bounded-read assertions passed ===
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: `readProviderJsonResponse` threw the canonical overflow error when JSON body exceeded 16 MiB. Server-side `socket.bytesWritten` confirmed ~20 MiB was sent before cancellation.
- **What was not tested**: live image generation API call (the proof exercises the same production SDK helper against the same shape of attack); cross-platform Node differences.

## Out of scope

- `extensions/googlechat/src/` — covered by separate PR (#3e, #96772).
- `src/video-generation/dashscope-compatible.ts` — has 2 more unbounded `response.json()` calls; could be a follow-up if needed.

## Risk

- **Very low**: swap from `response.json()` to `readProviderJsonResponse`. Error behavior preserves the same upstream catch path — the helper throws `${label}: malformed JSON response` on parse errors, which is equivalent to the old JSON syntax error.
- **Byte cap (16 MiB, default)**: invisible to normal-sized image responses (typically < 1 MiB). Only oversized/bodies trigger the new boundary.
- **Existing SDK helper**: `readProviderJsonResponse` has been in `openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-http` since the Alix-007 bounded-read sweep, used by 15+ providers.

## Diff stats

```
2 files changed, 11 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)
```

Net code change (excl. import line + mock): **1 line** (the actual call site swap).
